text: Phase system refactor: phases don't have start and end times. and then they just line up. User advances the 'cursor' on each level.
description: Refactor phases from time-based scheduling to an explicitly ordered hierarchy with per-level cursor navigation. Each depth is conceptually one virtual list formed by concatenating the children of all phases in the parent level, not only the selected parent, with non-selectable separators marking boundaries between parent-owned child ranges. Canonical persistence should use `meta.rootPhaseIds` for root ordering and `phase.childPhaseIds` for child ordering, while each phase keeps `parent: string | null` as a backlink. The active phase exists only in the focused level; other levels keep remembered selected positions. Navigating with j/k updates the active level, can move across parent boundaries within the same level, recursively updates parent selections up to root when crossing into items owned by different parents, and auto-scrolls related levels to keep the selected-path item near center. Child selection should remember per-parent child index when possible and fall back to a valid in-bounds index if the child set changes while away. Empty child ranges still expose a selectable placeholder item so o/O can create the first subphase; in that empty case o and O behave the same because they both create the first child. Windowing should be implemented with virtual scrolling and enough overflow before/after the viewport to cover at least 50% of page height. Migration from the old date-based phase model must be lazy and compatibility-preserving: legacy phases remain readable, initial order is derived from prior temporal ordering when needed, and persisted data is upgraded incrementally as phases or project metadata are touched rather than through a mandatory one-shot migration.
